item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1
I would personally want to own this object if it was real. It's a fun idea, and it's a much nicer SCP compared to all the ones that kill/eat people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the last note really makes thus one work. I like that the Foundation clearly has some hard lines they won't cross here. Ill second Octagonic that the tests are a little barebones but its not enough for me to downvote. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one. Sweet and simple, and potentially very powerful, but handled sensibly by the Foundation. I thought the mistakes made in tests were pretty sensible, stuff that could actually result from oversights in the testing process rather than pure stupidity. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I was thinking about adding something at the end about the researcher going through it himself due to the testing being halted but it just ended up being all over the place… I also considered having the chaos insurgency try to steal it, but none of it really fit into the article w/o feeling forced… I might revisit those ideas in a tale, but as of now I don't have any plans to edit it.
Thanks for the feedback though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I still think short, simple scips have their place. The effect was legitimately interesting.

By the way, in the third test you identify the D-class as female and then refer to them with masculine pronouns.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My main problem with the article is the lack of a narrative. The concept is interesting enough, it just needs some backstory and story progression to really carry the article, as most successful articles these days have a reason why they happen, or a hint at it at least. The descriptions of the tests also seem a bit lackluster in my opinion, as they always revolve around a D-Class getting sent back home and doing some incredibly over the top thing that would definitely register on the Foundations radar.

The clinical tone is pretty on point, along with formatting and SPaG. It just needs more of a narrative to drive it forward.

-1
you've also got an XXXX in your footnotes.

item.title: Here we go
item.content:encoded:
Alright, first SCP. I got feedback from the draft forum and edited it accordingly; time to put this thing out to pasture.

By no means feel obligated to, but if you downvote, I'd appreciate you posting your reasoning in this discussion thread.
